title: Advisory Remediation Progress Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Tracks Cisco PSIRT security advisories affecting managed devices, providing evidence of vulnerability awareness and remediation tracking as required by NIST 800-53 Flaw Remediation.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates continuous monitoring of vendor-published security advisories for all managed network infrastructure.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search over index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory scheduled daily; advisory counts and affected device lists exported to compliance dashboard.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Contributes to vulnerability scanning by tracking known vendor-published vulnerabilities, though it does not replace active scanning tools.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides a vendor-authoritative vulnerability feed for network infrastructure to complement active vulnerability scanning.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Dashboard panel showing advisory severity distribution and affected device counts, refreshed with each TA poll cycle.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Tracks remediation progress of technical vulnerabilities by comparing current vs historical affected device counts.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that identified vulnerabilities are being actively remediated over time.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Trending report showing advisory affected-device counts decreasing over 30-day windows; exported monthly for management review.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory`. from the `securityadvisory` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/security-advisory/advisory`. Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per advisory-device pair. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `advisoryId` (string), `advisoryTitle` (string), `severity` (string), `cveId` (string), `deviceCount` (integer), `deviceId` (string), `fixedVersions` (array of strings), `publicationDate` (string). CIM data model: Vulnerabilities.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" | stats dc(deviceId) as current_affected by advisoryId, severity, advisoryTitle | appendcols [search index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" earliest=-30d latest=-7d | stats dc(deviceId) as previous_affected by advisoryId] | eval remediation_pct=if(previous_affected>0, round((previous_affected-current_affected)*100/previous_affected,1), "N/A") | where current_affected > 0 | sort severity -current_affected
description: Tracks the remediation progress of security advisories over time by comparing current affected device counts against historical baselines.
value: Vulnerability remediation is a process, not a one-time event. Tracking progress demonstrates active management and identifies stalled remediation efforts.
implementation: Enable the `securityadvisory` input. `appendcols` requires aligned `advisoryId` order between the two `stats` outputs; for production, consider a `join` on `advisoryId` or `lookup` of precomputed baselines. Validate that subsearch time bounds cover representative weeks.
visualization: Table (advisoryId, severity, current_affected, previous_affected, remediation_pct), column chart of remediation_pct.
knownFalsePositives:
**Remediation progress appearing to regress when new devices are added to Catalyst Center.** When new devices are onboarded, they may be affected by existing advisories, increasing the `current_affected` count and making it appear that remediation progress has regressed. Distinguish by separating newly onboarded devices from the existing fleet: track `dc(deviceId)` over time and correlate with inventory additions. Suppress by computing remediation progress as a percentage of the original affected fleet, excluding devices onboarded after the advisory was first detected.

**Device remediated by firmware upgrade but compliance re-scan not yet complete.** A device that has been upgraded to a fixed version may still appear as affected until Catalyst Center's next PSIRT scan re-evaluates it. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim"` or `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:device"` for version changes. Suppress by allowing one scan cycle (3600s) after a device upgrade before counting it as still-affected.

**Compensating control applied but device still listed as affected.** Catalyst Center tracks affected devices by software version, not by compensating controls. A device with an ACL or IPS rule mitigating the vulnerability will still appear in the affected list. Distinguish by cross-referencing with the `catalyst_advisory_exceptions` lookup. Do not suppress from the advisory view — note the compensating control status alongside the advisory.

**Advisory with no available fix showing zero remediation progress.** Some advisories affect devices running software where no fixed version is available yet. Remediation progress will remain at 0% indefinitely. Distinguish by checking the advisory's `fixedVersions` — if empty, no remediation is possible. Suppress by separating advisories with available fixes from those without in the remediation tracking dashboard.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **securityadvisory** modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory`.
• At least 14 days of historical advisory data to establish a remediation baseline.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE**.
• SWIM (Software Image Management) data available for firmware upgrade correlation.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Remediation progress tracking correlates advisory data with device firmware versions over time. The TA polls the security advisory API, which includes the current `deviceCount` for each advisory.

Key fields:
• `advisoryId`: advisory identifier.
• `deviceCount`: current count of affected devices.
• `severity`: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW.
• `fixedVersions`: software versions that remediate the vulnerability.
• `_time`: when the poll occurred — track `deviceCount` over time to measure remediation progress.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH") deviceCount>0
| bin _time span=1d
| stats latest(deviceCount) as current_affected earliest(deviceCount) as initial_affected latest(severity) as severity by advisoryId, _time
| eventstats earliest(initial_affected) as baseline_affected by advisoryId
| eval remediated_pct=round((baseline_affected-current_affected)/baseline_affected*100,1)
| eval days_open=round((now()-_time)/86400,0)
| stats latest(current_affected) as current latest(baseline_affected) as baseline latest(remediated_pct) as pct_remediated max(days_open) as days_since_detection by advisoryId, severity
| sort severity, -current
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")`**: Focuses remediation tracking on the highest-risk advisories.
• **`bin _time span=1d`**: Daily granularity for tracking remediation progress. Daily snapshots of `deviceCount` show whether remediation is progressing.
• **`eventstats earliest(initial_affected) as baseline_affected`**: Captures the initial affected device count when the advisory was first detected — this is the denominator for remediation percentage.
• **`remediated_pct`**: How many devices have been remediated as a percentage of the initial affected count. 100% = fully remediated.
• **`days_since_detection`**: How many days the advisory has been tracked — used for SLA compliance.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** In Catalyst Center > Assurance > Security Advisories, select a specific advisory and check the affected device count. Compare with the Splunk `current_affected` value.
• **Remediation progress check:** After upgrading a device to a fixed version, verify the `deviceCount` decreases in the next advisory scan (up to 1 hour).

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Remediation progress chart showing `remediated_pct` over time for each critical advisory. Add SLA markers (e.g., 30-day CRITICAL remediation target, 90-day HIGH target).
• **Alert:** Trigger when a CRITICAL advisory has <50% remediation after 30 days, or a HIGH advisory has <50% after 90 days.
• **Executive reporting:** Monthly remediation status report showing advisories open, remediated, and SLA compliance.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Remediation progress appears to regress:** New devices onboarded to Catalyst Center may be affected by existing advisories, increasing `current_affected`. Track new device onboarding separately.
• **Advisory with no available fix showing 0% progress:** Separate advisories with `fixedVersions` from those without. Track the latter in a risk-acceptance dashboard.
• **Device upgraded but still showing as affected:** Catalyst Center's advisory scan runs periodically (up to 1 hour). Wait for the next scan cycle after the upgrade.
• **No securityadvisory events arriving:** verify the `securityadvisory` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **Missing fields or zero values in severity:** some advisory fields are only populated for certain PSIRT types; NULL values for `fixedVersions` indicate no patch is available.
• **Fewer records than expected:** the PSIRT scan may still be running for large inventories; if credential access is denied, check the service account permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We track how quickly your team is fixing security vulnerabilities by comparing how many devices are still affected compared to last month.
